title: BIG: Abolitionist Candidate Advances to Runoff Against GOP Chair Who Kept Abortion Legal in Texas
author: James Silberman
orientation: Free the States reports that abolitionist David Lowe forced incumbent State Rep. Stephanie Klick into a May 24 runoff in the Texas House District 91 Republican primary, framing the race as a referendum on Klick's role in killing the Abolition of Abortion in Texas Through Equal Protection Act and rallying the Abolitionist Movement to support Lowe.
audiences: abolitionist activist deciding where to volunteer
donor weighing whether to fund Lowe's runoff

questions.question: What was the result of the March 2022 Republican primary in Texas House District 91?
questions.answer: Incumbent Stephanie Klick finished first with 48.96 percent but failed to clear the 50 percent threshold needed to avoid a runoff, while abolitionist challenger David Lowe took second with 39.06 percent, sending the race to a May 24 runoff.
questions.quote: Texas House District 91 abolitionist challenger David Lowe advanced to a runoff against incumbent State Rep. Stephanie Klick in Tuesday's Republican Primary, garnering 39.06 percent of the vote. Klick received 48.96 percent, short of the 50 percent plus one vote needed to advance to the general election.

questions.question: Why does Free the States hold Stephanie Klick personally responsible for abortion remaining legal in Texas?
questions.answer: As chair of the Texas House Public Health Committee, Klick refused to give the Abolition of Abortion in Texas Through Equal Protection Act so much as a hearing during the 2021 session, blocking the bill that would have ended abortion in the state.
questions.quote: Klick, as chair of the Texas House Public Health Committee, refused to even give the Abolition of Abortion in Texas Through Equal Protection Act a hearing in the 2021 session. She is the reason babies are still murdered in Texas.

questions.question: How did Lowe perform against Klick despite a major spending disadvantage?
questions.audience: donor weighing whether to fund Lowe's runoff
questions.answer: Even though Klick outspent Lowe by roughly three to one, she still couldn't avoid a runoff, suggesting Lowe's grassroots reach is punching well above his fundraising weight.
questions.quote: She was unable to advance despite outspending Lowe by a roughly 3:1 margin.

questions.question: What historical precedent does Free the States cite for second-place candidates winning runoffs?
questions.answer: The article points to Warren Hamilton's 2020 race, in which he trailed incumbent Larry Boggs 42.21 to 46.45 in the first round but then jumped to 52.30 percent and won the runoff outright.
questions.quote: In 2020, abolitionist Warren Hamilton received 42.21 percent compared to incumbent Republican Larry Boggs' 46.45. But in the primary, Warren increased his vote share to 52.30, defeating Boggs handily.

questions.question: Why does Free the States compare the Lowe-Klick race to the 2020 Garvin-Scott race in Oklahoma?
questions.answer: The first-round numbers were nearly identical, and Garvin came back from a similar deficit to defeat the incumbent in the runoff, providing a non-abolitionist precedent that the same dynamics can flip a race.
questions.quote: Though neither is an abolitionist, their numbers in the first primary vote are nearly identical to the Lowe-Klick race. Scott finished in first with 49.89 percent, just 14 votes shy of winning the race outright. Garvin finished in second place with 39.26 percent. But in the runoff, Garvin overtook Scott to win 51.56-to-48.44.

questions.question: What policy commitments did Lowe make to the abolitionist movement?
questions.audience: abolitionist activist deciding where to volunteer
questions.answer: Lowe pledged to Free the States and Abolish Abortion Texas that he would back immediate abolition of abortion and reject the incrementalist strategy of the Pro-Life Establishment.
questions.quote: Lowe has pledged to Free the States and Abolish Abortion Texas that he will support the immediate abolition of abortion and reject the incrementalism of the Pro-Life Establishment.

questions.question: What is Free the States asking abolitionists to do between the March primary and the May 24 runoff?
questions.audience: abolitionist activist deciding where to volunteer
questions.answer: They are asking the Abolitionist Movement to mobilize behind Lowe and help educate District 91 voters on the contrast between Klick's pro-life incrementalism and Lowe's abolitionism over the seven weeks leading up to the runoff.
questions.quote: Lowe vs. Klick is the most important race in Texas, and he is going to need the support of the Abolitionist Movement if he is going to gain the ground he needs to gain over the next seven weeks! Lowe, Free the States, Abolish Abortion Texas, and Texas abolitionists will be working hard to educate District 91 voters about the difference between the pro-life incrementalism of Klick vs the abolitionism of David Lowe between now and May 24.

questions.question: How did the other minor candidates in the District 91 primary perform?
questions.answer: Anthony Reed pulled 6.22 percent, David Silvey 3.37 percent, and Benjamin Damico 2.40 percent, leaving roughly 12 percent of the vote up for grabs in the runoff.
questions.quote: After Klick and Lowe, Anthony Reed finished with 6.22 percent, David Silvey received 3.37 percent, and Benjamin Damico finished in fifth with 2.40 percent.

related.title: Warren Hamilton Wins
related.reason: The Hamilton-Boggs runoff is the lead historical precedent the article cites for an abolitionist second-place finisher overtaking an incumbent.

related.title: Two Important Notes on Jessica Garvin Unseating Paul Scott
related.reason: The article uses the Garvin-Scott numbers as a near-mirror of the Lowe-Klick spread, so this companion piece deepens that comparison.

related.title: 110,000 Lives Hang in the Balance as Abolition of Abortion in Texas Act Gets April 8th Hearing
related.reason: Background on the very Equal Protection Act that Klick later refused to grant a hearing in 2021.

related.title: State Rep. Bryan Slaton Files Bill to Immediately Abolish Abortion in Texas
related.reason: Establishes the abolition bill that Klick's committee killed and that Lowe pledged to support.

related.title: State Rep. Bryan Slaton Refiles Texas Abolition Bill in Special Session
related.reason: Continued legislative context for the Texas abolition fight that defines Lowe's platform and Klick's record.

related.title: Five Reasons Abolitionists Oppose Incremental Pro-Life Bills
related.reason: Unpacks the abolitionism-versus-incrementalism distinction the article tells District 91 voters they need to learn.

related.title: Watch: Wesley Thomas Brilliant Testimony at the TX GOP State Convention
related.reason: Same Texas-GOP-versus-abolitionists arena the Lowe campaign is fighting in, useful for activists framing their pitch.
